finding.description: A ctDNA-guided approach reduced adjuvant chemotherapy use without compromising recurrence-free survival.

source.title: Circulating Tumor DNA Analysis Guiding Adjuvant Therapy in Stage II Colon Cancer.

finding.description: The percentage of patients receiving adjuvant chemotherapy was lower in the ctDNA-guided group (15%) compared to the standard-management group (28%).
finding.effect_summary: Lower adjuvant chemotherapy use in ctDNA-guided group compared to standard management.

gpt_claim.source_quote: A lower percentage of patients in the ctDNA-guided group than in the standard-management group received adjuvant chemotherapy (15% vs. 28%; relative risk, 1.82; 95% confidence interval [CI], 1.25 to 2.65).

source.title: Circulating Tumor DNA Analysis Guiding Adjuvant Therapy in Stage II Colon Cancer.

finding.description: 2-year recurrence-free survival was 93.5% in the ctDNA-guided group and 92.4% in the standard-management group.
finding.effect_summary: Noninferior 2-year recurrence-free survival in ctDNA-guided group compared to standard management.

gpt_claim.source_quote: In the evaluation of 2-year recurrence-free survival, ctDNA-guided management was noninferior to standard management (93.5% and 92.4%, respectively; absolute difference, 1.1 percentage points; 95% CI, -4.1 to 6.2 [noninferiority margin, -8.5 percentage points]).

source.title: Circulating Tumor DNA Analysis Guiding Adjuvant Therapy in Stage II Colon Cancer.

finding.description: 3-year recurrence-free survival was 86.4% among ctDNA-positive patients who received adjuvant chemotherapy and 92.5% among ctDNA-negative patients who did not.
finding.effect_summary: Higher 3-year recurrence-free survival in ctDNA-negative patients compared to ctDNA-positive patients.

gpt_claim.source_quote: Three-year recurrence-free survival was 86.4% among ctDNA-positive patients who received adjuvant chemotherapy and 92.5% among ctDNA-negative patients who did not.

finding.effect_summary: No significant difference in 5-year OS between ctDNA-guided and standard management.

source.title: Circulating tumor DNA analysis guiding adjuvant therapy in stage II colon cancer: 5-year outcomes of the randomized DYNAMIC trial.

finding.description: 5-year recurrence-free survival was 88% with ctDNA-guided management and 87% with standard management.
finding.effect_summary: No significant difference in 5-year RFS between ctDNA-guided and standard management.

gpt_claim.source_quote: At a median follow-up of 59.7 months, 5-year RFS was 88% and 87% with ctDNA-guided and standard management, respectively (difference 1.1%, 95% confidence interval -5.8% to 8.0%).

source.title: Circulating tumor DNA analysis guiding adjuvant therapy in stage II colon cancer: 5-year outcomes of the randomized DYNAMIC trial.

finding.description: ctDNA clearance was observed at the end of ACT in 87.5% of treated ctDNA-positive patients.
finding.effect_summary: High rate of ctDNA clearance at the end of ACT in treated ctDNA-positive patients.

gpt_claim.source_quote: For treated ctDNA-positive patients, ctDNA clearance was observed at the end of ACT (EOT) in 35 out of 40 patients (87.5%).

source.title: Circulating tumor DNA analysis guiding adjuvant therapy in stage II colon cancer: 5-year outcomes of the randomized DYNAMIC trial.

finding.description: Higher than median postoperative tumor-derived mutant molecules per milliliter plasma was associated with worse 5-year RFS.
finding.effect_summary: Higher ctDNA levels post-surgery linked to worse 5-year RFS.

gpt_claim.source_quote: A higher than median postoperative tumor-derived mutant molecules per milliliter plasma was associated with worse 5-year RFS (HR 10.62; P = 0.005).

source.title: Circulating tumor DNA analysis guiding adjuvant therapy in stage II colon cancer: 5-year outcomes of the randomized DYNAMIC trial.

finding.description: Post hoc analysis showed ctDNA clearance at EOT predicted a 5-year recurrence-free probability of 97% versus 0% for ctDNA persistence.
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA clearance at EOT predicts high 5-year RFS.

gpt_claim.source_quote: Post hoc analysis of ctDNA clearance at EOT assessed by a new assay that evaluated an average of 29 tumor-derived mutations per patient predicted for a favorable 5-year recurrence-free probability of 97% versus 0% for ctDNA persistence (P < 0.001).

source.title: Molecular residual disease and efficacy of adjuvant chemotherapy in patients with colorectal cancer.

finding.description: Postsurgical ctDNA positivity at 4 weeks after surgery was associated with higher recurrence risk in patients with stage II or III CRC.
finding.effect_summary: Higher recurrence risk with postsurgical ctDNA positivity.

gpt_claim.source_quote: In this cohort, with a median follow-up of 16.74 months (range 0.49-24.83 months), postsurgical ctDNA positivity (at 4 weeks after surgery) was associated with higher recurrence risk (hazard ratio (HR) 10.0, P < 0.0001) and was the most significant prognostic factor associated with recurrence risk in patients with stage II or III CRC (HR 10.82, P < 0.001).

source.title: Molecular residual disease and efficacy of adjuvant chemotherapy in patients with colorectal cancer.

finding.description: Patients with stage II or III CRC and postsurgical ctDNA positivity derived benefit from ACT.
finding.effect_summary: Benefit from ACT in ctDNA-positive patients.

gpt_claim.source_quote: Furthermore, postsurgical ctDNA positivity identified patients with stage II or III CRC who derived benefit from ACT (HR 6.59, P < 0.0001).

source.title: Molecular residual disease and efficacy of adjuvant chemotherapy in patients with colorectal cancer.

finding.description: 18-month DFS was 38.4% for ctDNA-positive patients versus 90.5% for ctDNA-negative patients.
finding.effect_summary: Inferior DFS for ctDNA-positive patients.

gpt_claim.source_quote: Among 187 patients who were ctDNA positive 4 weeks after surgery, 61.4% (115 out of 187) experienced recurrence, whereas only 9.5% (81 out of 852) of patients who were ctDNA negative 4 weeks after surgery experienced recurrence (hazard ratio (HR) 10.0, 95% confidence interval (CI) 7.7–14.0, P < 0.0001), demonstrating an 18-month DFS of 38.4% (95% CI 31.4–45.5%) versus 90.5% (95% CI 88.3–92.3%), respectively.

source.title: Molecular residual disease and efficacy of adjuvant chemotherapy in patients with colorectal cancer.

finding.description: No significant benefit from ACT in ctDNA-negative patients.
finding.effect_summary: No significant benefit from ACT in ctDNA-negative patients.

gpt_claim.source_quote: In ctDNA-negative patients, after accounting for potentially confounding factors (age, sex, pathological stage, MSI and performance status), no statistically significant benefit from ACT was observed (adjusted HR 1.71, 95% CI 0.80–3.7, P = 0.167).

source.title: Molecular residual disease and efficacy of adjuvant chemotherapy in patients with colorectal cancer.

finding.description: ACT was associated with a higher cumulative incidence of ctDNA clearance.
finding.effect_summary: Higher ctDNA clearance with ACT.

gpt_claim.source_quote: In this analysis, ACT was associated with a higher estimated cumulative incidence of ctDNA clearance in 68.48% (63 out of 92) of patients by week 24 after surgery compared with 12.2% (11 out of 90) of patients who did not receive ACT (adjusted HR 8.50, 95% CI 4.2–17.3, P < 0.0001; HR was adjusted for sex, age, stage, MSI and performance status).

source.title: Molecular residual disease and efficacy of adjuvant chemotherapy in patients with colorectal cancer.

finding.description: Patients who converted from ctDNA negative to positive had a significantly higher risk of recurrence.
finding.effect_summary: Higher recurrence risk for patients converting from ctDNA negative to positive.

gpt_claim.source_quote: Compared with the risk of recurrence for patients who were persistently negative, a significantly higher risk of recurrence was observed for patients who converted from ctDNA negative to positive (HR 14.0, 95% CI 8.5–24.0, P < 0.001), with an 18-month DFS of 33.8% (95% CI 18.1–50.2%).

source.title: Molecular residual disease and efficacy of adjuvant chemotherapy in patients with colorectal cancer.

finding.description: The American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) panel states that the current definition of ‘high-risk’ stage II cancer is inadequate.

gpt_claim.source_quote: The American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) panel states that the current definition of ‘high-risk’ stage II cancer is inadequate as many patients with high-risk features do not experience recurrence, whereas some patients with average-risk features do.

finding.description: The median disease-free survival was 9.9 months with atezolizumab, compared to 4.8 months with placebo.
finding.effect_summary: Atezolizumab significantly improved disease-free survival compared to placebo.

gpt_claim.source_quote: The median disease-free survival was 9.9 months with atezolizumab, as compared with 4.8 months with placebo (hazard ratio for first event of disease recurrence or death, 0.64; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.47 to 0.87; P=0.005).

source.title: ctDNA-Guided Adjuvant Atezolizumab in Muscle-Invasive Bladder Cancer.

finding.description: The median overall survival was 32.8 months with atezolizumab, compared to 21.1 months with placebo.
finding.effect_summary: Atezolizumab significantly improved overall survival compared to placebo.

gpt_claim.source_quote: The median overall survival was 32.8 months with atezolizumab, as compared with 21.1 months with placebo (hazard ratio for death, 0.59; 95% CI, 0.39 to 0.90; P=0.01).

source.title: ctDNA-Guided Adjuvant Atezolizumab in Muscle-Invasive Bladder Cancer.

finding.description: Disease-free survival was 95% at 1 year and 88% at 2 years among patients with persistent ctDNA-negative status.
finding.effect_summary: High disease-free survival rates among ctDNA-negative patients.

gpt_claim.source_quote: Among 357 patients with persistent ctDNA-negative status, disease-free survival was 95% at the end of the 1-year monitoring period and 88% at 2 years.
